title: Atomic awareness refresh + SQLite connection lifecycle discipline
current_state: Two distinct correctness bugs grouped because the fixes are tiny and adjacent in scope. (a) pi_agent.py:264-290 awareness refresh has a TOCTOU gap between the TTL check and the thread spawn â€” two threads passing the TTL check simultaneously both set _awareness_refreshing=True and both spawn refresh threads. (b) pi_agent.py:1203-1215 _check_reminders opens a sqlite3 connection but only closes it inside the loop's finally â€” if an exception is raised BEFORE the loop body executes, conn.close() is skipped. (c) agent/watchers.py:41-45 _db() returns a raw open connection; callers expect to use it with a 'with' statement but it isn't a context manager, so 'with _db(path) as conn' relies on conn.__exit__ behaviour which only commits/rollbacks but does NOT close the connection.
structural_problem: Connection leaks accumulate over a long-running session and cause 'database is locked' under concurrent access. Awareness refresh races spawn redundant work and can corrupt the cached snapshot if both writers complete at the same time.
target_state: (a) Awareness refresh flag-set + thread-spawn inside the same lock; only the cache read is outside. (b) _check_reminders uses 'with closing(sqlite3.connect(...))' so the connection is guaranteed closed on any exit path. (c) _db() returns contextlib.closing(conn) so 'with _db(path) as conn' both auto-commits AND auto-closes.
design_notes: (a) Awareness refresh fix â€” pattern: 'with self._awareness_lock: if not stale: return cached; if self._awareness_refreshing: return cached; self._awareness_refreshing = True; threading.Thread(target=self._do_refresh, daemon=True).start()' â€” flag AND spawn inside lock
(a) self._do_refresh must clear self._awareness_refreshing = False in a finally block
(b) Use 'from contextlib import closing' at top of pi_agent.py if not already imported
(b) Replace 'conn = sqlite3.connect(...)' / 'conn.close()' pattern with 'with closing(sqlite3.connect(...)) as conn:' â€” single block, exception-safe
(c) Two options for _db() in watchers.py: (i) make it return contextlib.closing(conn), or (ii) rename to _connect() with explicit caller responsibility. Option (i) is less invasive â€” chosen.
(c) Audit caller sites in watchers.py: _init_db, every method that calls _db(). After change, 'with _db(path) as conn' will close on exit. Test that no caller assumes the raw connection
Add track_silent('db.connection_leak', e) inside the contextlib closing __exit__ if exception is the cause â€” surfaces silently leaked connections
success_criteria: 10 concurrent threads call self.awareness_snapshot() simultaneously when TTL expired â†’ only ONE refresh thread spawns (counter assertion)
_check_reminders raises a synthetic exception mid-method â†’ assert sqlite3 connection count returns to baseline within 100ms
watchers.py _db() callers all use 'with' syntax and connections are closed (test by stat'ing open file handles on Linux, or just trusting the contextlib closing on Windows)
risk_notes: Holding the awareness lock during thread spawn slightly increases contention. Mitigation: spawn is fast (microseconds); lock is uncontested 99.9% of the time. Risk on (c): callers that capture the connection beyond the 'with' block will break. Audit before/after.
migration_plan: 1. Awareness refresh fix in pi_agent.py:264-290: restructure as described in design_notes (a)
2. Ensure self._do_refresh() clears the flag in a finally â€” read current implementation to confirm; if missing, add it
3. _check_reminders fix: replace explicit close pattern with 'with closing(sqlite3.connect(self.memory.sqlite_path)) as conn:'
4. Import 'from contextlib import closing' at top of pi_agent.py if not present
5. watchers.py:41-45: change _db() return to 'return closing(sqlite3.connect(path, ...))' so callers using 'with _db(path) as conn:' get auto-close
6. Audit all _db() callers in watchers.py â€” grep '_db(' â€” confirm each uses 'with'. If any callers capture the raw connection, refactor.
7. Add testing/test_awareness_atomic.py: spawn 10 threads, count refresh-thread creations via patching threading.Thread; assert == 1
8. Add testing/test_db_connection_lifecycle.py: simulate exception in _check_reminders body; assert no leaked connection (use weakref / counter on sqlite3.connect)
test_plan: test_awareness_single_refresh_under_contention â€” 10 threads parallel call â†’ only 1 refresh thread started
test_awareness_flag_cleared_on_exception â€” patch _do_refresh to raise; assert _awareness_refreshing returns to False
test_check_reminders_no_leak_on_exception â€” patch a method called inside the loop to raise; track sqlite3.connect callers; assert all closed
test_watchers_db_helper_auto_closes â€” open 100 connections via _db(); after their 'with' blocks; assert all closed (mock or fd counter)
